text:
Once upon a time, in a small town nestled amongst towering mountains, there lived a quirky old bookstore owner named Herman. His shop was filled with oddities—a ticking clock, a unicorn statue, and a dictionary that spelled backward.

Every weekend, Herman invited his favorite books to hang out. His favorite was "Alice's Adventures in Wonderland." One peculiar day, the books decided they wanted company. They played tag through the halls, danced to carousel music because why not, and even held a tea party with Herman.

But one rainy afternoon, as Herman sat reading under an oak tree, a mysterious stranger approached. This stranger was unlike anyone Herman had ever seen. He wore layers of darkness, had sharp ears, and carried a book bag that smelled of incense.

"Greetings," the stranger said cryptically. "I'm Looking Glass."

Herman exchanged amused glances but said nothing.

"You've lots of cool books," Looking Glass continued. "I love stories!"
